responses: Hello! Ask me about any disease or symptoms.
Malaria is caused by parasites transmitted by mosquitoes. Symptoms include fever, chills, and sweating.
Diabetes is a condition where blood sugar levels are too high due to insulin problems.
Hypertension is a condition where blood pressure is consistently too high.
Typhoid is a bacterial infection spread through contaminated food and water.
Asthma is a condition where airways become inflamed and narrow.
Tuberculosis is a bacterial infection that mainly affects the lungs.
COVID-19 is a viral infection affecting the respiratory system.
Flu is a viral infection causing fever, cough, and body aches.
Pneumonia is an infection that inflames air sacs in the lungs.
Ulcers are sores that develop on the stomach lining.
Migraine is a neurological condition causing intense headaches.
Arthritis causes inflammation and pain in joints.
Anemia occurs when you lack enough healthy red blood cells.
Cholera is a bacterial infection causing severe diarrhea.
Hepatitis is inflammation of the liver, often caused by viruses.
Meningitis is inflammation of the membranes around the brain and spinal cord.
Cancer is a disease where abnormal cells grow uncontrollably.
Stroke occurs when blood supply to part of the brain is interrupted.
Heart disease refers to various conditions affecting the heart.
Kidney disease affects the kidneys' ability to filter waste.
Bronchitis is inflammation of the bronchial tubes.
Eczema is a condition that makes skin itchy and inflamed.
Psoriasis causes skin cells to build up and form scales.
Depression is a mental health disorder causing persistent sadness.
Anxiety is a mental health condition involving excessive fear.
Goodbye! Stay healthy.
